danger_signals.visual_form: Karate students and thugs on screen
Health meter reduction visible on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Player taking damage; proximity to game-over state

danger_signals.visual_form: Named character (Johnny Lawrence, Chozen) with increased energy bar
danger_signals.meaning: Significant combat challenge; stage progression gate

danger_signals.visual_form: Flying objects (sticks, birds) during typhoon stage; swinging hammer; ice blocks
danger_signals.meaning: World-state danger independent of enemy combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Girl on pole (Level 3); Kumiko on ground beside boss (Level 4)
danger_signals.meaning: Secondary objective failure state; rescue mechanic activates danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies in Level 4 require two hits instead of one; spear-wielding enemies require more hits
danger_signals.meaning: World difficulty escalation; late-stage threat elevation

safety_signals.visual_form: Large 'C' and 'D' symbols dropping from defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Crane Kick and Drum Punch abilities acquired; temporary advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: C and D symbols restore low amount of Daniel's energy meter
Ice-blocks to break, flies to catch with chopsticks, hammers to dodge
safety_signals.meaning: Optional skill-building reduces vulnerability; world depth signal

safety_signals.visual_form: Kumiko present and safe beside Daniel
safety_signals.meaning: Objective success state; stage completion condition met

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition between tournament, Okinawa, typhoon, and festival settings
safety_signals.meaning: Player advancement; narrative safety milestone reached

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Karate gi (white training outfit, new outfit in Level 4)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: All Valley Karate Tournament setting (Level 1); Okinawa dojo locations (Levels 2-3); festival (Level 4)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Increasing energy bar per opponent faced in Level 1
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament arena setting; controlled dojo environment

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct from karate students; casual clothing implied
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Low durability (one hit to defeat) in Level 2; increased durability (two hits) in Level 4
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Okinawa streets and public spaces; later festival grounds

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Named boss character; repeated encounters signal faction identity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears as Level 2 boss, Level 3 boss (rescue mechanic), Level 4 boss (with Kumiko hostage)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Animated head appearance in ending sequence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Absent from playable stages; present only in victory state communication

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All Valley Karate Tournament arena; structured opponent progression; energy bar increases per round
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player fights series of karate students in escalating difficulty; one-on-one combat focus

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Okinawa setting; random thugs spawning; training opportunities embedded (ice, flies, hammers)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies die in one hit; player collects C/D power symbols; mandatory skill training available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Strong wind effects on player jumps; flying objects (sticks, birds) traverse screen as environmental hazards; stage layout nearly identical to Level 2
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World actively damages player independent of enemies; rescue mission for girl on pole; Chozen fight optional (rescue is primary objective)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Festival setting; Daniel in new outfit (visual costume change); enemy durability doubled; spear-wielding enemies introduced; Kumiko present in boss arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final level threat increase; Chozen battle mandatory with secondary rescue mechanic; failure state if Kumiko falls off platform and drowns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mr. Miyagi animated head appearance; speech confirmation ('You have successfully guided Daniel-san'); character wink gesture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game loop completion; narrative closure; player recognized as 'martial arts master'

environmental_storytelling.example: Level 1 (tournament) → Level 2-3 (Okinawa) → Level 4 (festival) mirrors film plot structure; geography change signals story advancement
Typhoon in Level 3 manifests as wind effects and flying objects; communicates danger without character dialogue; visual-only storytelling

environmental_storytelling.technique: NPC rescue mechanics as moral stakes
environmental_storytelling.example: Girl on pole (Level 3) and Kumiko on platform (Level 4) represent non-combat objectives; drowning mechanic in final stage elevates stakes narratively
Daniel's new outfit in Level 4 visually indicates growth/advancement; cosmetic change without gameplay alteration reinforces narrative development

environmental_storytelling.technique: Optional training activities as world lore
environmental_storytelling.example: Breaking ice-blocks, catching flies with chopsticks, dodging hammers embed dojo training philosophy in gameplay; communicate 'Miyagi method' without exposition
Chozen appears three times with changing roles (primary opponent → auxiliary to rescue → final boss with hostage); visual recycling communicates persistent threat

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Energy bar increases per opponent; each successive student represents higher threat; final opponent presumably Johnny Lawrence
Die in one hit; spawn randomly; drop power-up symbols (C/D) at regular intervals
Require two hits to defeat; increased durability signals world-state change; spear-wielding variant requires additional hits
Represents stage gate; must be defeated to advance; named antagonist signals faction identity
Present but defeat optional; girl-on-pole rescue takes narrative priority; behavior change due to environmental state (rescue > combat)
Kumiko at his side creates dual-objective state; player must combat Chozen while maintaining ally proximity; ally drowning creates failure state independent of combat loss
Visual transformation signals mastery progression; no mechanical change but signals narrative advancement to player
Enable special moves (Crane Kick, Drum Punch); replenish energy; communicate resource economy and progression system to player

notes: The Karate Kid NES employs minimal narrative exposition, relying heavily on visual setting changes, environmental hazards, and NPC presence to communicate world-state and stakes. The game uses costume changes, enemy durability scaling, and rescue mechanics as primary storytelling tools. Factions are defined by location and opponent type rather than distinct visual markers (no faction colors or complex sigils). Environmental storytelling emphasizes physical training (ice-breaking, fly-catching, hammer-dodging) as cultural/narrative world-building. The ending sequence's Mr. Miyagi appearance serves as ultimate safety signal—victory confirmation delivered through character cameo rather than menu state.
